channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: DJ Clue Talks J. Cole, Clipse & Trying to Get Jay-Z Back in the Studio: ‘I Got a Whole Archive of Songs I’ve Never Used’
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: Welcome to our podcast, where we dive into the world of music and catch up with the industry's biggest names. Today, we're talking about a legend in the game - DJ Clue. With a career spanning decades, Clue has been making waves in the music scene since the '90s. From his iconic mixtapes to his recent collaborations with top artists, Clue is back in the spotlight.
[Music transitions to a clip of DJ Clue's signature drops]
Host: You might remember Clue's famous drops - "Cluminati", "Clue, Clue, Clueee", and of course, the evil laugh. These catchy phrases have become synonymous with the influential DJ from Queens. But Clue's career is more than just his memorable drops. He started out in the '90s, flooding the streets with his mixtapes and eventually signing with Jay-Z's Roc-A-Fella Records.
[Music transitions to a clip of J. Cole's "Birthday Blizzard '26" EP]
Host: Recently, Clue has seen a resurgence in popularity, thanks in part to his collaboration with J. Cole on the "Birthday Blizzard '26" EP. The EP features four freestyles over classic Clue tape instrumentals, including Diddy's "Victory" and Biggie's "Who Shot Ya?". The project has been well-received by fans and fellow artists alike, with Jay-Z and Travis Scott both giving it a shoutout.
[Music transitions to an interview clip with DJ Clue]
Host: We caught up with DJ Clue at our New York City office to talk about his recent collaborations and what's next for the legendary DJ. Clue shared with us how the collaboration with J. Cole came about, and how he had to dig up old beats and instrumentals to make it happen.
[Music transitions to a clip of DJ Clue talking about his process]
Host: Clue also teased that he's working on something new, but kept the details under wraps. With a career spanning decades, Clue has seen the music industry change and evolve, but his influence remains strong. We're excited to see what he has in store for us next.
[Outro music plays]
Host: That's all for today's episode. Thanks for tuning in, and we'll catch you on the next one.
